subsystem.function: Flight controller/autopilot tray, telemetry and VTX modules, motor power vs signal wire separation across tilt joint

instruction: Design only this subsystem: P0 plan, part_tree children, 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops. Every evidence_feature must appear. Do not modify other subsystems or the global envelope.

criteria.title: Electronics, Signal, and Motor Wiring Judgment

criteria.good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
criteria.bad: Power and signal wires share one tight bundle along the whole route with no separation or shielding note.
criteria.loop_feedback: Split power and signal into separate raceways or add spacing/shielding notes where they must share a channel.

criteria.good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.
criteria.bad: Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add a named ground/bond feature per module and state how each signal's return path is routed.

criteria.good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.
criteria.bad: Boards are floated or glued in cavities with no standoffs, clearance, or connector orientation logic.
criteria.loop_feedback: Mount each board on standoffs with stated keep-out clearance and orient connector edges toward access openings.

criteria.good: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
criteria.bad: P0 lists features but geometry_ops are generic boxes at the origin.

criteria.good: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume.
criteria.bad: Geometry is stacked at [0,0,0] or lacks coordinates.

criteria.good: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer — not just boxes.
criteria.bad: Only box and cylinder ops with no subtractive or finishing features.

criteria.good: Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops (body+subtract+interface+finish).
criteria.bad: Some children have 0-1 ops (LOW-RES). All detail concentrated on primary body.

criteria.good: A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed.
criteria.bad: No datum reference — assembly alignment is uncontrolled.

criteria.good: Adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm.
criteria.bad: Interfaces have no tolerance or fit specification.

criteria.good: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
criteria.bad: No assembly order — build sequence is ambiguous.

criteria.good: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, pocket, drill, subtract. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present.
criteria.bad: Only basic box/cylinder ops. Missing subtractive or finishing features typical for this seed.

criteria.good: Dimensions match tiltrotor scale: 200-1500mm wingspan, 50-300mm fuselage dia. Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible.
criteria.bad: Dimensions are placeholder (all 10mm) or unrealistic for the subsystem scale.

citable_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook SP-2016-6105 Rev2
1800 Mechanical Movements, Devices and Appliances (Hiscox, 1899, public domain)

boundary_note: Subsystem scale: 200-1500mm wingspan, 50-300mm fuselage dia. Environment: outdoor flight, -20 to +50C, rain/dust/salt. Design loads: rotor thrust 20-200N, tilt servo 5-30Nm, 3g maneuvering.

operability_profile.operating_principle: VTOL aircraft architecture that transitions thrust vector through nacelle tilt axes while preserving wing load paths, slack-loop harnesses, and control authority.
